page.content: In the Infrastructure Investment Report for FY2023, total investment amounts to $24,000,000, representing 100% of the budget, with an overall rating of B+ and challenges faced including regulatory delays. The report categorizes investments as follows: Campus Facilities received $5,000,000, marked as completed with an A rating; Online Education, $3,000,000, classified as in progress; Technology Upgrades, $2,000,000, also in progress; Student Housing, $8,000,000, completed with an A+ rating; Library Renovation, $1,000,000, in progress with a B+ rating; Sports Facilities, $4,000,000, planned with a B rating; Research Labs, $6,000,000, completed with an A- rating; IT Infrastructure, $2,500,000, in progress; Green Initiatives, $1,500,000, planned with a B rating; and Transport Services, $1,200,000, completed with an A rating.

lines.text: In the Infrastructure Investment Report for FY2023, total investment amounts to $24,000,000, representing 100% of the budget,
with an overall rating of B+ and challenges faced including regulatory delays. The report categorizes investments as follows:
Campus Facilities received $5,000,000, marked as completed with an A rating; Online Education, $3,000,000, classified as in
progress; Technology Upgrades, $2,000,000, also in progress; Student Housing, $8,000,000, completed with an A+ rating;
Library Renovation, $1,000,000, in progress with a B+ rating; Sports Facilities, $4,000,000, planned with a B rating; Research
Labs, $6,000,000, completed with an A- rating; IT Infrastructure, $2,500,000, in progress; Green Initiatives, $1,500,000,
planned with a B rating; and Transport Services, $1,200,000, completed with an A rating.

page.content: The budget allocation stands at 30%, with future projections indicating growth potential. In the enrollment section, undergraduate programs in Engineering have 1,200 students, a growth rate of 15%, and revenue of $3M, categorized as active in Singapore. Business programs enroll 800 students with a 10% growth rate and $2M revenue, also active in Australia. Arts programs, with 400 students, show a 5% growth and $1M revenue, active in New Zealand. Health Sciences enroll 600 students, with a 12% growth and $1.5M revenue, active in Malaysia, while IT programs have 1,000 students, a 20% growth rate, and $2.5M revenue, active in Thailand.
